7190	  morphisms, \(F(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{F(Y)} = 0 = F(0)\); faithfulness of \(F\)
7191	  then makes the map on hom-sets injective, so \(\mathrm{id}_Y = 0\), whence \(Y\) is a
7192	  zero object.

7410	      because the only such package available is the \emph{simplicial} one producing a homotopy equivalence
7411	      of \emph{chain} complexes (face maps), whereas the augmented {\v C}ech complex here is a \emph{cochain}
7412	      complex (coface maps); there is no cosimplicial/cochain dual to invoke.
7413	  \end{enumerate}
7414	
7415	  \emph{Step 4: the augmentation node.} The augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to
7416	  \mathcal{C}^0\) is handled by the \emph{same} prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy of Step 3. Over an
7417	  open \(V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the contracting homotopy extends to the augmentation term:
7418	  the degree-\((-1)\)/degree-\(0\) component of \(h\) sends a section \(s \in \prod_s \mathcal{F}(U_s
7419	  \cap V)\) to its \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-component \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}
7420	  \cap V) = \mathcal{F}(V)\), so the identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\) holds at the
7421	  augmentation node as well. In the formalization mechanism above this is the degree-\(0\) instance: the
7422	  same per-degree identification (b) and the same homotopy (c) cover the augmentation term, the
7423	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-component map \(s \mapsto s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\) landing in
7424	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V) = \mathcal{F}(V)\). Thus the augmented section complex over each
7425	  such \(V\) is exact at degree
7426	  \(0\) (and the augmentation is the kernel inclusion there). Sheafifying this local exactness as in
7427	  Step 3 makes the augmentation-node homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of
7428	  the augmented complex is zero, so the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of
7429	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\). In
7430	  particular the statement holds for an arbitrary \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) and an
7431	  arbitrary open cover; quasi-coherence and affineness are needed only by the downstream consumers
7432	  (termwise \(f_*\)-acyclicity), not by this exactness.
7433	
7434	  \emph{Remark.} An alternative proof uses the stalk-at-prime criterion directly: the complex is
7435	  exact if and only if it is exact after localizing at each prime \(\mathfrak{p}\), and for each
7436	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) one fixes an index \(i_{\text{fix}}\) with \(f_{i_{\text{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\)
7437	  and defines a contracting homotopy of the localized complex by
7438	  \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\), as in the Stacks source proof above.
7439	  This approach is equivalent to the sections-and-sheafification argument of Steps 1--4.
7440	\end{proof}

7442	\begin{lemma}
7443	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
7444	  \label{lem:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7445	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
7446	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
7447	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
7448	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
7449	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
7450	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
7451	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
7452	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
7453	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
7454	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
7455	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
7456	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
7457	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
7458	  \[
7459	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
7460	      \;\cong\;
7461	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
7462	  \]
7463	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
7464	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
7465	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
7466	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
7467	\end{lemma}
7468	
7469	\begin{proof}
7470	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
7471	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
7472	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
7473	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
7474	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
7475	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
7476	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
7477	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
7478	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
7479	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
7480	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
7481	  additive homology-preserving functor.
7482	\end{proof}
